title: Lellingeria subcoriacea
taxonomy: Lellingeria subcoriacea is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, which is a family of ferns. This plant belongs to the genus Lellingeria and is further classified as a species under that genus. The classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and genetic analysis. Lellingeria subcoriacea is a type of epiphytic fern that grows in tropical regions. It is a member of the class Polypodiopsida, which is a division of vascular plants.

intro: Lellingeria subcoriacea, also known as the Subcoriaceous Lellingeria, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, native to Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Lellingeria subcoriacea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Lellingeria subcoriacea is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin issues.
